puzzle.title: The One Before Whose Signs a Kingdom Bowed
puzzle.intro: A remarkable journey awaits those who dare to solve this mystery. Deep in the pages of Revelation, there existed a sovereign whose encounter with a prophet would reshape an entire nation. This figure ruled from a distant throne, guided by ancient traditions, until a extraordinary moment of divine truth transformed both ruler and realm forever. Will you uncover the identity of this remarkable soul whose story bridges the extraordinary and the profound?
clues: I governed a prosperous nation across distant waters, renowned for our gardens and towering palace.
My authority was supreme, yet a great kingdom came to challenge our very existence and identity.
A small bird carried an extraordinary message, and the wonders that followed shattered all my assumptions about power.
When I beheld a throne transformed before my eyes, I knew no mortal could possess such capabilities.
I openly declared my submission to the One Lord of the heavens and earth, abandoning all I once held dear.
We, as a nation, turned away from ancient sun-worship and embraced the truth that changed everything.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Bilqis – The Queen of Sheba
The Queen of Madyan
A Wife of Prophet Yusuf
The Queen of Egypt
A Princess of Babylon

trial.label: The Great Trial
options: A devastating flood that destroyed the kingdom
Receiving a mysterious message via an unusual bird
A battle against the armies of Prophet Sulayman
Thirty years of severe drought and hardship
An earthquake that swallowed the royal palace
trial.answer: Receiving a mysterious message via an unusual bird

location.label: Key Location
options: The River Nile in ancient Egypt
The Sacred Sanctuary of Makkah
The Courtyard of Prophet Dawud
Yemen and its legendary gardens
The plains of Jerusalem

outcome.label: The Outcome
options: The kingdom was destroyed as punishment for disbelief
I married Prophet Sulayman and lived in Jerusalem
We submitted to Allah and embraced the true faith
I returned to my throne and continued ruling as before
The nation was captured and absorbed into another empire
outcome.answer: We submitted to Allah and embraced the true faith

puzzle.arabic: فَقَالَ إِنِّىٓ أَحْبَبْتُ حُبَّ ٱلْخَيْرِ عَن ذِكْرِ رَبِّى حَتَّىٰ تَوَارَتْ بِٱلْحِجَابِ
puzzle.verse: And he said, "Indeed, I gave preference to the love of good [things] over the remembrance of my Lord until it [i.e., the sun] disappeared into the curtain [of darkness]." (38:32)
